items.title: 'Kick in the guts': NT veterans join calls for health funding cap removal

children.content: The federal government wants to cap access to physical and mental health supports to $5,000 a year for injured war veterans, but there are growing concerns the move will push some into "a worse-off situation".

gallery.altText: A group of people sitting around a table in a cafe, an older man is pointing his finger as he speaks to the group.

thumbnail.altText: A group of people sitting around a table in a cafe, an older man is pointing his finger as he speaks to the group.

items.title: Court finds staffer's relationship with 20yo parolee 'grossly inappropriate'

children.content: An Alice Springs parole worker found in a hotel room with the 20-year-old she was responsible for supporting has avoided prison for attempting to pervert the course of justice.

image.altText: A woman outside the court hides her face behind a cardigan, supporters around her attempt to block the view with umbrellas.

items.title: NT Police says public has no need to be concerned about new officer class

children.content: The Northern Territory Police Force has moved to reassure Territorians that new, armed public safety officers due to hit the beat from today are fully trained to carry guns.

image.altText: Police officers in uniform converse.

items.title: Jillaroos, jackaroos and an iconic event: The Brunette Races in pictures

children.content: More than 800 people from across northern Australia hit a remote outback cattle station over the weekend for the iconic annual Brunette Races.

articles.title: Young NT cattle ringers conquer outback obstacle course

children.content: Based on the day in the life of an outback station ringer, the Battle of the Barkly obstacle course has been won by two young cattle industry workers.

image.altText: Man celebrates winning a competition while doing a lap of honour on a desert horse racing tack

A man in a large black cowboy hat pulls a saddle apart
A cowboy on top of a brown horse against a black night sky mid air as he is bucked from his horse.

items.title: Thousands watch on as boats made of beer cans race on Darwin beach

children.content: Competitors at the annual Beer Can Regatta race rafts engineered from drinking containers, with beer or soft-drink cans the popular choice this year.

image.altText: Group of people watch a beer can boat race at the beach

items.title: Darwin vacation care worker charged with indecently touching child

children.content: NT Police allege the male educator, 40, indecently touched a child under the age of 16 at a vacation care program in Darwin.

image.altText: An NT Police Car parked on the street.

items.title: A speeding shadow minister puts the brakes on NT Labor’s rebuild

children.content: Revelations about an NT shadow minister’s dicey driving history — and the Opposition Leader's failure to disclose it — come as another blow to NT Labor.

image.altText: an aboriginal man wearing a suit in parliament

items.title: While teaching about pancreatic cancer, John realised he ticked every box

children.content: Clinical worker John McNeur didn't realise he was unwell until he was delivering a health workshop on pancreatic cancer in the remote Northern Territory — and he began ticking all of the boxes. Now he's on a mission to raise awareness.

image.altText: A close-up portrait of a bald man looking straight at the camera

items.title: The artefacts that tell the story of one of Australia's most notorious cases

children.content: Forty years ago, the chance discovery of a bloodied matinee jacket unravelled a murder conviction. Now, that evidence lives inside a national museum's collection that's too sensitive for public display.

gallery.altText: A museum curator wearing blue surgical gloves, holding a box which has a baby girl's pink outfit laid flat inside.
A small, dirty knit jacket laid flat on tissue paper.
A black and white photo of a couple outside court, they are holding up a large photo of Lindy Chamberlain holding baby Azaria.

thumbnail.altText: A museum curator wearing blue surgical gloves, holding a box which has a baby girl's pink outfit laid flat inside.
A small, dirty knit jacket laid flat on tissue paper.
A black and white photo of a couple outside court, they are holding up a large photo of Lindy Chamberlain holding baby Azaria.

items.title: Midwife hit with $70,000 foreign investment tax after moving to Australia

children.content: A British midwife who migrated to Australia to work at a regional hospital during statewide staff shortages is calling for changes to the law after being slugged $70,000 by state and federal authorities for the purchase of her home.

gallery.altText: A British midwife with brown hair stands next to her husband in front of their home in Whyalla

thumbnail.altText: A British midwife with brown hair stands next to her husband in front of their home in Whyalla

items.title: Simpson Desert has never looked better but where are the tourists?

children.content: Birdsville, a pilgrimage point for outback travellers, navigates the ripple effects of flood, fuel fears and the cancellation of a massive music festival, when the surrounding country has rarely looked so green and alive.

image.altText: A sandbar overlooking flooded desert landscape.

items.title: Seven Australians in Venezuela request government help as quake toll climbs

children.content: Australia is providing "consular assistance" to seven people after Venezuela's two earthquakes, Foreign Affairs Minister Penny Wong confirms.

image.altText: an aerial image of apartment buildings reduced to rubble

items.title: More Australians are doing their own tax returns and making these mistakes

children.content: With side hustles, working from home, crypto investments, renting out rooms and receiving income through digital platforms or apps, experts say people are at greater risk of making errors with their tax.

gallery.altText: woman looking at laptop on the couch with paperwork

thumbnail.altText: woman looking at laptop on the couch with paperwork

avatar.alt: Kellie Scott black and white headshot

items.title: Treasurer 'willing to engage' on super for under-18s

children.content: Treasurer Jim Chalmers says he's open to discussing a guarantee for superannuation to be paid for teenage workers but stresses that's not the government's focus at the moment. Follow live.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit standing in front of an Australian flag.

items.title: Are the wheels falling off the AI investment boom?

children.content: Overshadowing the tumult that is global politics has been a financial boom beyond almost anything witnessed before, focused entirely on the perceived benefits wrought by a brave new world of AI that threatens to up-end our lives.

image.altText: AI imagery with large blue letters on a dark blue base, saying AI

items.title: New fellowship for mid-career journalists honours Judith Whelan

children.content: The ABC, The Sydney Morning Herald and Sydney University have created a new fellowship to honour the late Judith Whelan, a trailblazing leader in media.

image.altText: A blonde woman smiling with black round glasses.

items.title: Sons remember 'loving' mum after Airbnb guest found guilty of killing her

children.content: Maree Vermont's sons say they never thought hosting Airbnb guests would be dangerous for their mother, who was proud of the home she had built.

image.altText: Two men stand in front of a stone house with signs of fire damage.

items.title: Ill-fated midnight mountain walk in freezing conditions led to 12-day search

children.content: The NSW coroner praises emergency services for "persistence" during the 12-day search for a hiker who set off on an ill-fated midnight mountain walk in sub-zero temperatures while wearing sandals.

image.altText: A woman with blond hair smiling at the camera.

items.title: 'We don't fully understand': The footy risk few parents grasp

children.content: Contact is an essential part of footy, but there is growing evidence it can also be a grave threat to the health and welfare of its players. Experts say the AFL should be acting now to protect current and future players.

gallery.altText: Players compete for the ball at a junior Australian Rules football match.

thumbnail.altText: Players compete for the ball at a junior Australian Rules football match.

avatar.alt: Portrait of ABC journalist Jessica Halloran.

items.title: BalletTok, blackface and nudes: Inside the world of professional ballet

children.content: Ariane Beeston, the author of an acclaimed memoir detailing her experience with postpartum psychosis, has penned her first work of fiction, Don't Forget to Smile.

image.altText: Woman with long dark hair sitting wearing white and looking at camera

items.title: Will you raise the white flag on these tricky flag questions?

children.content: How many stars are there on the US flag? And how many stars are on the Europe flag?

image.altText: The Australian, Aboriginal and Torres Strait flags fly in Canberra.

items.title: Multiple injuries, driver unaccounted for in truck-stop crash inferno

children.content: Explosions, multiple injuries and up to seven semi-trailers destroyed as a truck collision sets off a fiery chain of events on the Hume Highway.

image.altText: A fireball caused by a burning truck in the dark.

gallery.altText: rfs truck with blaze behind it on highway road

items.title: Strait of Hormuz strikes likely to disrupt shipping again

children.content: Fresh attacks on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz have exposed the fragility of the 60-day ceasefire deal between the US and Iran, which was meant to allow ships to transit through the waterway again.

image.altText: A drone view of vessles anchored in the Strait of Hormuz as seen from Musandam, Oman

items.title: Social media giants face fines for failing to hand over documents

children.content: Labor wants to double the fines for tech giants who refuse to hand over documents that could prove they are failing to enforce Australia’s social media age ban.

image.altText: A woman in an burnt orange suit, gestures with her hands while standing at a lectern.

items.title: NDIS shell company boom sparks crackdown

children.content: The federal government says it is moving to crack down on the growing trade in "off-the-shelf" NDIS providers.

thumbnail.altText: A middle aged white woman with short fair hair and red suit sitting in an office chair

items.title: Court rejects Trump's final bid to overturn E Jean Carroll verdict

children.content: The US Supreme Court declined to hear US President Donald Trump's bid to overturn a $5 million USD verdict in a case in which a jury found him liable for sexually abusing and defaming E Jean Carroll.

image.altText: A woman with blonde hair and orange tinted glasses

items.title: ASX trading sideways, Wall Street jumps as AI-related stocks rebound

children.content: The Australian share market has started its day relatively flat despite a rebound in oil prices ahead of the next round of US-Iran peace talks. On Wall Street, the Dow Jones Industrial Average hit a record high. Follow live.

image.altText: Picture of stock market board with ASX sign at the top left corner.

items.title: Multi-million-dollar giveaway club banned by NSW regulator

children.content: One of Australia's biggest "rewards clubs" has been banned from running any gaming activity in New South Wales for 18 months after allegedly breaking the law.

thumbnail.altText: A luxurious house on the gold coast with a large pool, surrounded by palm trees.

items.title: The medicine safety system most Australians have never heard of

children.content: A little black triangle on medicine information leaflets is meant to make it easier to report adverse effects of new medications. Problem is, many Australians don't know what it means.

image.altText: A pile of drugs on a table, all are different shapes and sizes.

items.title: Homemade production of 'lethal weapons' on the rise

children.content: Since Queensland strengthened its firearm laws in March, one person a fortnight has been charged with possessing a blueprint to make 3D-printed guns.

image.altText: an open shoulder bag with several glocks inside

items.title: Bondi terror attack survivor received 'influx of hate' hours after shooting

children.content: Deepfake and altered images depicting a Bondi Beach terror attack survivor holding an Academy Award while covered in blood spread "like wildfire" online just hours after the shooting, a royal commission has heard.

items.title: New measures coming to NSW include end to 'one of the biggest rip-offs'

children.content: Motorists, public transport users and wage earners are the biggest winners under new changes across NSW starting from July 1.

image.altText: A wallet with an NAB and Qantas card and a $5 and $10 notes poking out

items.title: ABC presenter Trevor Chappell to hang up mic after 26 years

children.content: After decades of keeping night owls and shift workers company on ABC radio across Australia, Overnights presenter Trevor Chappell decides to call it a day at the age of 65.

image.altText: Overnights presenter Trevor Chappell wears a blue shirt and stands near the arts centre

items.title: Police hold 'real concern' about anonymous letters sent to politicians

children.content: Police have asked for the community's assistance to identify the author targeting parliamentarians and religious organisations, who goes by 'Scorpio' or 'Bullit'.

image.altText: A composite of a scary face with 'Scorpio' written underneath and capital letters underlined in red on the right.

items.title: Six killed in German 'family tragedy' shooting, say police

children.content: Two people have been arrested after a shooting in the northern German town of Stade.
